scenario: A team wants to call the same DMN decision from multiple BPMN processes. They wonder if this is supported and best practice.
question: Can a DMN decision be invoked from multiple BPMN processes?

options.text: Yes -- DMN decisions are deployed cluster-wide and can be invoked by any BPMN process via Business Rule Task with decisionId reference. Single source of truth for the decision logic; reused across processes. Best practices: - Stable decisionId across versions (treat as public interface). - Backward-compatible changes when evolving (don't break callers). - Document the decision's inputs / outputs for caller awareness. - Test changes against representative callers.

DMN decisions in Camunda 8 are deployed cluster-wide and referenced by their decision ID. Any number of BPMN processes can configure a Business Rule Task pointing to the same decisionId, and each invocation runs independently without shared state. Restricting a decision to a single caller would negate the primary architectural benefit of centralized decision logic and force teams to maintain duplicate rules across separate DMN files.

Duplicating the DMN file once for each BPMN process that needs it is an anti-pattern that violates the DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principle. When the rule logic needs to change, every duplicated copy must be found and updated individually -- any missed copy causes inconsistency between processes. Bug fixes in one copy do not propagate to others. Camunda 8 is specifically designed to allow multiple processes to call the same deployed decision by referencing a shared decisionId, making duplication unnecessary and harmful to maintainability.

Suggesting BKMs (Business Knowledge Models) as the mechanism for sharing decision logic across multiple BPMN processes is a misapplication. BKMs are a DMN-internal reuse mechanism -- they encapsulate sub-logic that can be called from within other decisions in the same DMN file. BKMs are not directly invokable from BPMN process instances. The correct approach for multi-process reuse is to deploy a shared decision and have each BPMN process reference it via a Business Rule Task with the shared decisionId.

a.text: Correct. DMN decisions, like BPMN processes, are deployed cluster-wide and referenced by ID. Multiple BPMN processes can invoke the same decision: Setup: 1. Deploy DMN with decision discount-calculator. 2. BPMN Process A: Business Rule Task with decisionId="discount-calculator". 3. BPMN Process B: Business Rule Task with same decisionId. 4. Each invocation evaluates the decision independently; results don't share state. Benefits: Single source of truth: one decision implementation; all callers use it. Consistency: updating the decision propagates to all callers' next invocations. Maintainability: centralised logic. Best practices: Stable interface (decisionId): don't change ID across versions; callers depend on it. Backward-compatible evolution: add inputs / outputs without removing existing ones. Communication: when changing the decision, notify caller process owners. Versioning awareness: decisions evolve; running instances of older BPMN versions may use older decision versions depending on resolution semantics. Testing: changes tested against representative input sets covering all callers' use cases. Anti-pattern: copy-pasting the same DMN logic into multiple files for separate callers -- diverges over time; bug fixes don't propagate; defeats DRY.

c.text: Incorrect. Option d) -- Partial. BKM is DMN-internal; multi-process reuse via decision invocation.
